name: compute
context: Create, configure, and manage Google Compute Engine VMs — instances, disks, images, and machine types
info: This skill covers the full Compute Engine lifecycle: creating VM instances with specific machine types, boot disks, and network configs; connecting via SSH; starting, stopping, and deleting instances; managing persistent disks and snapshots; and using instance templates and managed instance groups for scaling. All operations use the gcloud CLI or the Compute Engine REST API via client libraries. Agents use this skill to provision development environments, run batch workloads, host services, and manage infrastructure. See [[gcp/iam]] for IAM roles needed (roles/compute.admin, roles/compute.instanceAdmin.v1) and [[gcp/gcloud]] for CLI setup.
triggers: attach a disk to a gcp vm
set up a gcp virtual machine
body:
# Compute Engine

Google Compute Engine (GCE) provides scalable virtual machines running on Google's infrastructure. VMs support Linux and Windows, custom machine types, local SSDs, GPUs, and preemptible/spot pricing.

## Creating an Instance

```bash
# Create a basic instance
gcloud compute instances create my-vm \
  --zone=us-central1-a \
  --machine-type=e2-medium \
  --image-family=debian-12 \
  --image-project=debian-cloud \
  --boot-disk-size=50GB \
  --boot-disk-type=pd-balanced \
  --tags=http-server,https-server

# Create with a startup script
gcloud compute instances create web-server \
  --zone=us-central1-a \
  --machine-type=e2-standard-2 \
  --image-family=ubuntu-2404-lts-amd64 \
  --image-project=ubuntu-os-cloud \
  --metadata-from-file=startup-script=./startup.sh \
  --scopes=cloud-platform

# Create a spot (preemptible) instance for cost savings
gcloud compute instances create batch-worker \
  --zone=us-central1-a \
  --machine-type=n2-standard-4 \
  --provisioning-model=SPOT \
  --instance-termination-action=STOP \
  --image-family=debian-12 \
  --image-project=debian-cloud
```

## Machine Types

| Family | Example | vCPUs | Memory | Use Case |
|--------|---------|-------|--------|----------|
| E2 | e2-medium | 2 | 4 GB | General purpose, cost-effective |
| N2 | n2-standard-4 | 4 | 16 GB | Balanced compute |
| C3 | c3-highcpu-8 | 8 | 16 GB | Compute-intensive |
| M3 | m3-megamem-64 | 64 | 896 GB | Memory-intensive |
| A2/A3 | a2-highgpu-1g | 12 | 85 GB | GPU workloads (NVIDIA) |
| Custom | custom-4-8192 | 4 | 8 GB | Exact vCPU/memory specs |

```bash
# List available machine types in a zone
gcloud compute machine-types list --zones=us-central1-a

# Create a custom machine type
gcloud compute instances create custom-vm \
  --zone=us-central1-a \
  --custom-cpu=6 \
  --custom-memory=24GB
```

## SSH Access

```bash
# SSH into an instance (auto-generates keys if needed)
gcloud compute ssh my-vm --zone=us-central1-a

# SSH with a specific user
gcloud compute ssh user@my-vm --zone=us-central1-a

# Run a command remotely
gcloud compute ssh my-vm --zone=us-central1-a --command="uname -a"

# SCP files to/from an instance
gcloud compute scp ./local-file.txt my-vm:~/remote-file.txt --zone=us-central1-a
gcloud compute scp my-vm:~/remote-file.txt ./local-copy.txt --zone=us-central1-a
```

## Instance Lifecycle

```bash
# List instances
gcloud compute instances list

# Stop an instance (still billed for disk)
gcloud compute instances stop my-vm --zone=us-central1-a

# Start a stopped instance
gcloud compute instances start my-vm --zone=us-central1-a

# Delete an instance
gcloud compute instances delete my-vm --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

# Describe instance details
gcloud compute instances describe my-vm --zone=us-central1-a --format=json
```

## Persistent Disks

```bash
# Create a persistent disk
gcloud compute disks create data-disk \
  --zone=us-central1-a \
  --size=200GB \
  --type=pd-ssd

# Attach a disk to an instance
gcloud compute instances attach-disk my-vm \
  --disk=data-disk \
  --zone=us-central1-a

# Create a snapshot
gcloud compute disks snapshot data-disk \
  --zone=us-central1-a \
  --snapshot-names=data-disk-snap-$(date +%Y%m%d)
```

## Firewall Rules

```bash
# Allow HTTP traffic
gcloud compute firewall-rules create allow-http \
  --direction=INGRESS \
  --action=ALLOW \
  --rules=tcp:80 \
  --target-tags=http-server

# Allow HTTPS traffic
gcloud compute firewall-rules create allow-https \
  --direction=INGRESS \
  --action=ALLOW \
  --rules=tcp:443 \
  --target-tags=https-server

# Allow SSH from specific IP
gcloud compute firewall-rules create allow-ssh-office \
  --direction=INGRESS \
  --action=ALLOW \
  --rules=tcp:22 \
  --source-ranges=203.0.113.0/24
```

## Python Client Library

```python
from google.cloud import compute_v1

def create_instance(project_id: str, zone: str, instance_name: str) -> compute_v1.Instance:
    client = compute_v1.InstancesClient()
    instance = compute_v1.Instance()
    instance.name = instance_name
    instance.machine_type = f"zones/{zone}/machineTypes/e2-medium"

    disk = compute_v1.AttachedDisk()
    disk.auto_delete = True
    disk.boot = True
    init = compute_v1.AttachedDiskInitializeParams()
    init.source_image = "projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12"
    init.disk_size_gb = 50
    disk.initialize_params = init
    instance.disks = [disk]

    network_interface = compute_v1.NetworkInterface()
    network_interface.name = "global/networks/default"
    access = compute_v1.AccessConfig()
    access.name = "External NAT"
    access.type_ = "ONE_TO_ONE_NAT"
    network_interface.access_configs = [access]
    instance.network_interfaces = [network_interface]

    op = client.insert(project=project_id, zone=zone, instance_resource=instance)
    op.result()  # Wait for completion
    return client.get(project=project_id, zone=zone, instance=instance_name)
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` | Default project ID |
| `CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_ZONE` | Default compute zone |
| `CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_REGION` | Default compute region |
| `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` | Path to service account key file |

sections.title: Creating instances
sections.body: Use `gcloud compute instances create NAME` with `--zone`, `--machine-type`, `--image-family`, and `--image-project`. Common image families: `debian-12` (debian-cloud), `ubuntu-2404-lts-amd64` (ubuntu-os-cloud), `centos-stream-9` (centos-cloud), `windows-2022` (windows-cloud). Set `--boot-disk-size` and `--boot-disk-type` (pd-standard, pd-balanced, pd-ssd). Add `--tags` for firewall targeting. Use `--metadata-from-file=startup-script=FILE` for boot-time configuration. See [[gcp/iam]] for required role: `roles/compute.instanceAdmin.v1`.

sections.title: Machine types and sizing
sections.body: GCE offers predefined machine types in families: E2 (cost-effective), N2/N2D (balanced), C3 (compute-optimized), M3 (memory-optimized), A2/A3 (GPU). Custom machine types let you specify exact vCPU/memory: `--custom-cpu=6 --custom-memory=24GB`. Use `gcloud compute machine-types list --zones=ZONE` to see available types. Spot VMs (`--provisioning-model=SPOT`) cost 60-91% less but can be preempted.

sections.title: SSH and file transfer
sections.body: Use `gcloud compute ssh INSTANCE --zone=ZONE` for SSH access — gcloud manages key generation and OS Login automatically. Transfer files with `gcloud compute scp LOCAL INSTANCE:REMOTE --zone=ZONE`. For tunneling: `gcloud compute ssh INSTANCE --zone=ZONE -- -L 8080:localhost:80`. OS Login (`--metadata=enable-oslogin=TRUE`) maps IAM identities to Linux accounts for centralized access control.

sections.title: Persistent disks and snapshots
sections.body: Create disks with `gcloud compute disks create NAME --size=SIZE --type=TYPE --zone=ZONE`. Disk types: pd-standard (HDD), pd-balanced, pd-ssd, pd-extreme. Attach to running instances with `gcloud compute instances attach-disk`. Inside the VM, format (`mkfs.ext4`) and mount the disk. Create snapshots for backups: `gcloud compute disks snapshot DISK --snapshot-names=NAME`. Snapshots are incremental and cross-regional.

sections.title: Networking and firewall
sections.body: Instances launch in the default VPC unless you specify `--network` and `--subnet`. Create firewall rules with `gcloud compute firewall-rules create` targeting instances by `--target-tags`. Use `--source-ranges` to restrict ingress by IP. For internal-only VMs, omit the external access config. Static external IPs: `gcloud compute addresses create NAME --region=REGION` then `--address=IP` on instance creation.

related.note: gcloud CLI must be installed and authenticated

scripts.description: Create, list, start, stop, or delete Compute Engine VM instances via gcloud CLI

depends.reason: gcloud CLI must be installed and configured
